Quinn Robinson

21

EscapeWorks Denver

This was so much fun! I've done this as a team building exercise and with a group of friends. You definitely have to like the people you are in the escape room with! You all will have to work together to beat the timer and escape the room!

HOME – GREAT DIVIDE BREWERY - GREAT DIVIDE ...

This is a great place in downtown Castle Rock to hangout with friends and have a great meal. I'm very partial to the Gyro with fries (some of the BEST fries)! I have it almost every time I'm there. They do have breakfast included on their lunch menu and they have a different dinner menu than lunch.<br><br>I was just here for dinner last night and had the Prime Dip! *chef's kiss* It was amazing!! And have I mentioned their fries??? 😉

The Rabbit Hole

This place is absolutely amazing! It's a must see if you're an Alice in Wonderland fan. It has great food and fabulous cocktails (try theWhite Rabbit)! I thought it was more of an upscale, fancier place, but last time I was there, some people were dressed more casually than others. Do plan to spend a bit more on your experience, but it's sooooo worth it!

Travel YOUNG! (before being swallowed by the 9-5)

If you're even considering traveling a little bit, I encourage you to peruse this list to see if you can find something that fits your agenda! I'm about to graduate college and I am NOT ready or willing to start working a traditional yet (maybe ever lol) so I am trying to gather all the travel opportunities I can for young adults. In a perfect world, I'd travel forever, but I do think its important to travel while we are young, healthy, and not tied to a lot of commitments yet. For young adults looking for gap year activities before or after college, or just looking for travel opportunities that they don't have to do alone!

Marketplace - Berkeley Parents Network

Berkeley Parents Network Marketplace (Berkeley and vicinity) is a moderated email newsletter of baby- and kid-related items up for grabs at usually good prices (Must subscribe to participate).

NHTSA

AAA will check your car seat installation for free or point you to a fire station near you that will do it. Click here (https://www.nhtsa.gov/campaign/safercargov?redirect-safercar-sitewide&q_State=&q_Zip=94608) to see the list of places to get car seat inspections and the phone numbers to make an appointment.

Classes – BirthWays

Birthways (Berkeley) is a non-profit that brings Childbirth Prep, Breastfeeding, Home Birth 101, and Meet the Doula nights to East Bay expectant parents. Browse their newborn classes here

Birth and Baby Resources

Thanks to East Bay Music Together for sponsoring this guide to resources for new parents and babies in the East Bay. Brydee Cummings

16

‎Google Maps

Google maps has a much better track record then Apple maps when abroad. I found it super reliable and accurate. SAFETY TIP: If you're travelling alone have an airpod in telling you your direction so you don't look confused and unaware on the streets! #girltravel

TunnelBear for iPhone & iPad

Okay this one, I didn't initially think it was important but I've come to decide that it's a NON-NEGOTIABLE app to have when travelling. TunnelBear protects your VPN - basically it protects your internet connection on any network. This is so important if your using a free or public Wi-Fi service (yes, even your hotel) or using an e-sim and doing something like online banking. Internet security is importtannntt so don't skip this one &lt;3

‎Google Translate

This one is a MUST HAVE! Whilst you should already know a few key phrases (hello, thankyou, excuse me) in your target language and English is widely spoken across SE Asia - you can't rely on this for accurate communication. Google translate can translate anything you type into it, say into it or even take a photo of (SO HELPFUL TO TRANSLATE MENUS!) and vice versa for whoever your speaking to.

See all 16 recs

Apps I will NEVER travel without

Every single app in this list is currently sitting in my ‘Travel’ folder on my phone.
